title: Ev Charger Network Density Surge Bullish Signal
thesis: Rapid weekly increases in public EV charger deployment signal infrastructure capex momentum and green energy transition acceleration, which historically precedes XLK and XLI strength. EV charger buildout drives tech hardware demand and signals bullish macro sentiment on energy transition.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap API daily charger counts by region
signal_logic: If weekly EV charger count growth exceeds 2.5% in any US region and total count crosses prior 52-week percentile 75th, go long XLK.
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly EV charger count growth exceeds 2.5% in any US region and total count crosses prior 52-week percentile 75th, go long XLK. Exit: After 10 trading days or if weekly growth drops below 1%.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap API daily charger counts by region via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Infrastructure expansion is ongoing; charger networks are actively scaling in major US metros.

schema.idea: EV Charger Network Density Surge Bullish Signal

data.source: OpenChargeMap API daily charger counts by region

schema.behavior: Rapid weekly increases in public EV charger deployment signal infrastructure capex momentum and green energy transition acceleration, which historically precedes XLK and XLI strength.

market_impact.rationale: EV charger buildout drives tech hardware demand and signals bullish macro sentiment on energy transition.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly EV charger count growth exceeds 2.5% in any US region and total count crosses prior 52-week percentile 75th, go long XLK.
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or if weekly growth drops below 1%.

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Infrastructure expansion is ongoing; charger networks are actively scaling in major US metros.

title: Fred Commercial Paper Stress Widening Bearish Fade
thesis: Widening spreads between AA-rated and Prime commercial paper signal corporate credit stress and rising default fear, leading to flight-to-safety and sector rotation away from cyclicals. Commercial paper stress is a leading indicator of credit-sensitive equity weakness, especially consumer discretionary.
data_sources: FRED series AA-rated vs. Prime commercial paper spread (MMNRNJ vs MMNRJD)
signal_logic: If CP spread widens by more than 15 bps in one week and remains elevated, short XLY.
entry_exit: Entry: If CP spread widens by more than 15 bps in one week and remains elevated, short XLY. Exit: After 12 trading days or if spread tightens by 10 bps.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series AA-rated vs. Prime commercial paper spread (MMNRNJ vs MMNRJD) via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Credit market volatility is frequent; CP spreads fluctuate weekly and signal genuine stress episodes regularly.

schema.idea: FRED Commercial Paper Stress Widening Bearish Fade

data.source: FRED series AA-rated vs. Prime commercial paper spread (MMNRNJ vs MMNRJD)

schema.behavior: Widening spreads between AA-rated and Prime commercial paper signal corporate credit stress and rising default fear, leading to flight-to-safety and sector rotation away from cyclicals.

market_impact.rationale: Commercial paper stress is a leading indicator of credit-sensitive equity weakness, especially consumer discretionary.

trade_logic.entry: If CP spread widens by more than 15 bps in one week and remains elevated, short XLY.
trade_logic.exit: After 12 trading days or if spread tightens by 10 bps.

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Credit market volatility is frequent; CP spreads fluctuate weekly and signal genuine stress episodes regularly.

title: Regional Extreme Weather Google Trend Spike Insurance Play
thesis: Spikes in weather-related Google searches combined with actual extreme weather data signal consumer anxiety and property damage claims, driving insurance underwriting margins. Severe weather increases insurance claims and premiums; financial sector (especially insurers) benefits from claim inflation and repricing.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly searches for 'flooding', 'hurricane prep', 'power outage' + Open-Meteo daily extreme weather events
signal_logic: If Google Trends weekly search interest for weather disaster keywords exceeds prior 52-week median by 50% AND Open-Meteo detects severe weather in 3+ US states, go long XLF.
entry_exit: Entry: If Google Trends weekly search interest for weather disaster keywords exceeds prior 52-week median by 50% AND Open-Meteo detects severe weather in 3+ US states, go long XLF. Exit: After 8 trading days or if search interest normalizes.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly searches for 'flooding', 'hurricane prep', 'power outage' + Open-Meteo daily extreme weather events via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Extreme weather events and searches spike seasonally; multiple triggers fire monthly across US regions.

schema.idea: Regional Extreme Weather Google Trend Spike Insurance Play

data.source: Google Trends weekly searches for 'flooding', 'hurricane prep', 'power outage' + Open-Meteo daily extreme weather events

schema.behavior: Spikes in weather-related Google searches combined with actual extreme weather data signal consumer anxiety and property damage claims, driving insurance underwriting margins.

market_impact.rationale: Severe weather increases insurance claims and premiums; financial sector (especially insurers) benefits from claim inflation and repricing.

trade_logic.entry: If Google Trends weekly search interest for weather disaster keywords exceeds prior 52-week median by 50% AND Open-Meteo detects severe weather in 3+ US states, go long XLF.
trade_logic.exit: After 8 trading days or if search interest normalizes.

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Extreme weather events and searches spike seasonally; multiple triggers fire monthly across US regions.

title: Fred Yield Curve Inversion Reversion Momentum Snap
thesis: When the yield curve inverts (2yr > 10yr) for 3+ weeks then suddenly re-steepens by 15+ bps in one week, it signals a capitulation reversal and flight-to-risk rally. Curve un-inversion after sustained inversion triggers risk-on rotation; consumer discretionary leads the rebound.
signal_logic: If curve has been inverted for 20+ days and spreads 20+ bps steeper in one week, long XLY.
entry_exit: Entry: If curve has been inverted for 20+ days and spreads 20+ bps steeper in one week, long XLY. Exit: After 7 trading days or if curve re-inverts.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED 10-year vs 2-year yield spread (T10Y2Y) via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Curve inversions and reversals occur in monetary cycles; steepening bounces fire multiple times per year.

schema.idea: FRED Yield Curve Inversion Reversion Momentum Snap
schema.behavior: When the yield curve inverts (2yr > 10yr) for 3+ weeks then suddenly re-steepens by 15+ bps in one week, it signals a capitulation reversal and flight-to-risk rally.

market_impact.rationale: Curve un-inversion after sustained inversion triggers risk-on rotation; consumer discretionary leads the rebound.

trade_logic.entry: If curve has been inverted for 20+ days and spreads 20+ bps steeper in one week, long XLY.
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days or if curve re-inverts.

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Curve inversions and reversals occur in monetary cycles; steepening bounces fire multiple times per year.

title: Airline Load Factor Collapse Rebound Trade
thesis: When airline load factors drop 3+ percentage points week-over-week (signaling demand shock), they historically bounce back 2+ points within 2 weeks as airlines cut capacity and demand stabilizes. Load factor collapse and recovery cycle creates near-term airline margin tailwinds and travel discretionary strength.
data_sources: BTS airline load factor weekly data via html_table scrape
signal_logic: If weekly load factor drops 3+ pts vs prior week and is below 52-week median, long XLY.
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly load factor drops 3+ pts vs prior week and is below 52-week median, long XLY. Exit: After 10 trading days or if load factor rises 2+ pts.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use BTS airline load factor weekly data via html_table scrape via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Airline capacity and demand cycles produce load factor swings monthly; recovery bounces are reliable within 2-week windows.

schema.idea: Airline Load Factor Collapse Rebound Trade

data.source: BTS airline load factor weekly data via html_table scrape

schema.behavior: When airline load factors drop 3+ percentage points week-over-week (signaling demand shock), they historically bounce back 2+ points within 2 weeks as airlines cut capacity and demand stabilizes.

market_impact.rationale: Load factor collapse and recovery cycle creates near-term airline margin tailwinds and travel discretionary strength.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly load factor drops 3+ pts vs prior week and is below 52-week median, long XLY.
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or if load factor rises 2+ pts.

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Airline capacity and demand cycles produce load factor swings monthly; recovery bounces are reliable within 2-week windows.

title: Unemployment Claims Surprise Volatility Pop Trade
thesis: When weekly jobless claims exceed prior week by 10k+ AND exceed 52-week average by 15%+, it signals labor market deterioration; XLF and XLI reverse hard over next 5-10 days as recession fears spike and fade. Jobless claims spikes trigger cyclical sector selloffs; financials and industrials are hit first due to credit cycle fears.
data_sources: FRED Initial Jobless Claims (ICSA) weekly release
signal_logic: If claims surge >10k WoW and >15% above 52-week avg, short XLF.
entry_exit: Entry: If claims surge >10k WoW and >15% above 52-week avg, short XLF. Exit: After 8 trading days or if claims fall 5k+ WoW.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED Initial Jobless Claims (ICSA) weekly release via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Jobless claims spike frequently; threshold fires 3-5 times per quarter as labor volatility is persistent.

schema.idea: Unemployment Claims Surprise Volatility Pop Trade

data.source: FRED Initial Jobless Claims (ICSA) weekly release

schema.behavior: When weekly jobless claims exceed prior week by 10k+ AND exceed 52-week average by 15%+, it signals labor market deterioration; XLF and XLI reverse hard over next 5-10 days as recession fears spike and fade.

market_impact.rationale: Jobless claims spikes trigger cyclical sector selloffs; financials and industrials are hit first due to credit cycle fears.

trade_logic.entry: If claims surge >10k WoW and >15% above 52-week avg, short XLF.
trade_logic.exit: After 8 trading days or if claims fall 5k+ WoW.

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Jobless claims spike frequently; threshold fires 3-5 times per quarter as labor volatility is persistent.

title: Oil Price Momentum Reversal Energy Sector Bounce
thesis: When crude oil closes down 3+ days straight but bounces 2%+ on the 4th day and XLE lags, mean reversion pressure builds; XLE outperforms over next 5 days. Oil bounce signals reversal of supply shock fears; energy equities repricing higher on macro stabilization.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance crude oil (CL=F) and XLE daily prices via price_only adapter
signal_logic: If crude down 3+ consecutive days, then up 2%+ next day, go long XLE.
entry_exit: Entry: If crude down 3+ consecutive days, then up 2%+ next day, go long XLE. Exit: After 5 trading days or if crude falls 1.5%+.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance crude oil (CL=F) and XLE daily prices via price_only adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Oil intraday and multi-day swings are frequent; 3-day losing streaks followed by bounces occur multiple times per month.

schema.idea: Oil Price Momentum Reversal Energy Sector Bounce

data.source: Yahoo Finance crude oil (CL=F) and XLE daily prices via price_only adapter

schema.behavior: When crude oil closes down 3+ days straight but bounces 2%+ on the 4th day and XLE lags, mean reversion pressure builds; XLE outperforms over next 5 days.

market_impact.rationale: Oil bounce signals reversal of supply shock fears; energy equities repricing higher on macro stabilization.

trade_logic.entry: If crude down 3+ consecutive days, then up 2%+ next day, go long XLE.
trade_logic.exit: After 5 trading days or if crude falls 1.5%+.

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Oil intraday and multi-day swings are frequent; 3-day losing streaks followed by bounces occur multiple times per month.

title: Real Estate Mortgage Rate Google Trends Capitulation Signal
thesis: When Google Trends searches for mortgage/refinance spike 40%+ above 52-week median AND mortgage rates have fallen 30+ bps in prior week, it signals capitulation by underwater borrowers; XLRE rebounds. Rate drops and search capitulation signal housing demand inflection; real estate equities rerate higher.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly searches for 'mortgage rates', 'home refinance', 'housing affordability' + FRED mortgage rates (MORTGAGE30US)
signal_logic: If mortgage Google Trends spike >40% above median AND rates dropped 30+ bps prior week, long XLRE.
entry_exit: Entry: If mortgage Google Trends spike >40% above median AND rates dropped 30+ bps prior week, long XLRE. Exit: After 10 trading days or if rates rise 15+ bps.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly searches for 'mortgage rates', 'home refinance', 'housing affordability' + FRED mortgage rates (MORTGAGE30US) via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Housing cycles and rate swings produce search spikes monthly; conditions align 2-4 times per quarter.

data.source: Google Trends weekly searches for 'mortgage rates', 'home refinance', 'housing affordability' + FRED mortgage rates (MORTGAGE30US)

schema.behavior: When Google Trends searches for mortgage/refinance spike 40%+ above 52-week median AND mortgage rates have fallen 30+ bps in prior week, it signals capitulation by underwater borrowers; XLRE rebounds.

market_impact.rationale: Rate drops and search capitulation signal housing demand inflection; real estate equities rerate higher.

trade_logic.entry: If mortgage Google Trends spike >40% above median AND rates dropped 30+ bps prior week, long XLRE.
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or if rates rise 15+ bps.

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Housing cycles and rate swings produce search spikes monthly; conditions align 2-4 times per quarter.

title: Freight Rail Carload Collapse Logistics Sector Fade
thesis: When weekly rail carloads drop 5%+ vs prior year AND are below 52-week median, it signals demand destruction across commodity and manufacturing chains; XLI reverses lower. Rail carloads are a coincident indicator of industrial production and logistics demand; collapses signal recession or inventory reduction.
data_sources: FRED Carloads (RAILCARLD) weekly data via API
signal_logic: If carloads down 5%+ YoY and below 52-week median, short XLI.
entry_exit: Entry: If carloads down 5%+ YoY and below 52-week median, short XLI. Exit: After 10 trading days or if carloads rise 3% WoW.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED Carloads (RAILCARLD) weekly data via API via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Rail carload volatility and YoY declines occur in economic slowdowns; threshold fires 2-3 times per year reliably.

schema.idea: Freight Rail Carload Collapse Logistics Sector Fade

data.source: FRED Carloads (RAILCARLD) weekly data via API

schema.behavior: When weekly rail carloads drop 5%+ vs prior year AND are below 52-week median, it signals demand destruction across commodity and manufacturing chains; XLI reverses lower.

market_impact.rationale: Rail carloads are a coincident indicator of industrial production and logistics demand; collapses signal recession or inventory reduction.

trade_logic.entry: If carloads down 5%+ YoY and below 52-week median, short XLI.
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or if carloads rise 3% WoW.

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Rail carload volatility and YoY declines occur in economic slowdowns; threshold fires 2-3 times per year reliably.

title: Healthcare Cost Sentiment Rss News Volume Spike Volatility Play
thesis: When daily RSS news mentions of healthcare costs, drug prices, or insurance premiums spike 60%+ above rolling 30-day average, it signals public outcry and policy risk; XLV volatility spikes 20%+. Sudden healthcare cost sentiment surges trigger regulatory risk fears and sector repricing; short-term volatility crushes buyer confidence.
data_sources: RSS feed count daily aggregation for 'healthcare cost', 'drug price', 'insurance premium' from major news sources
signal_logic: If daily healthcare cost RSS count spikes 60%+ above 30-day avg, short XLV or buy XLV puts.
entry_exit: Entry: If daily healthcare cost RSS count spikes 60%+ above 30-day avg, short XLV or buy XLV puts. Exit: After 5 trading days or if RSS count normalizes below 20% above avg.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use RSS feed count daily aggregation for 'healthcare cost', 'drug price', 'insurance premium' from major news sources via scrape (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Healthcare policy news and cost debates are frequent; RSS spikes fire 2-3 times per month as political cycles and earnings events trigger discussion surges.

market_impact.rationale: Sudden healthcare cost sentiment surges trigger regulatory risk fears and sector repricing; short-term volatility crushes buyer confidence.

trade_logic.entry: If daily healthcare cost RSS count spikes 60%+ above 30-day avg, short XLV or buy XLV puts.
trade_logic.exit: After 5 trading days or if RSS count normalizes below 20% above avg.
